_known_mend_defect.summary: UA's bundled in-process Apache Ant library does not recognise the `loaderid` attribute on <taskdef>. The attribute was added in Ant 1.8 (2010). The Ant version bundled in the bolt-4 SCM scanner pre-dates that.
_known_mend_defect.customer_impact: Any customer with build.xml using Ant 1.8+ syntax (specifically <taskdef loaderid=...>) cannot be scanned via SCM integration. No documented workaround: `ant` is not in install-tool's supported tool list, so scanSettings.versioning.ant is a no-op. The UA loads Ant as an in-process library, not a CLI binary, so no ant.environmentPath or similar knob exists. Customers must downgrade their build.xml syntax — which may not be feasible.

_mode_3_notes: The bolt-4 SCM scanner does NOT invoke Mode-1 Ivy resolution.
It falls through to Mode-3 default path scan: SHA1 fingerprints of binary files (jar/war/dll/etc).
Each lib/*.jar appears as a flat direct dependency with no transitive structure.
Dynamic-revision (rev='3.+') and changing-revision (rev='latest.integration' changing='true') semantics are exercised at lib-population time: JARs were pre-resolved to their concrete versions.
The probe validates Mend's binary fingerprinting + concrete version extraction — NOT Ivy resolution itself.
REGRESSION SIGNAL: Mend must emit version='3.14.0' for commons-lang3 (not '3.+') and version='1.10.0' for commons-text (not 'latest.integration').
Mode-1 testing of dynamic/changing semantics requires pipeline-mode 'mend ua -c whitesource.config', not SCM integration.

commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar._version_note: Concrete resolution of dynamic rev='3.+'. Mend MUST emit '3.14.0', NOT '3.+'.
commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar._dynamic_intent: rev='3.+' in ivy.xml — Ivy latest-3.x selector

commons-text-1.10.0.jar._version_note: Concrete resolution of changing rev='latest.integration' changing='true'. Mend MUST emit '1.10.0', NOT 'latest.integration'.
commons-text-1.10.0.jar._changing_intent: rev='latest.integration' changing='true' in ivy.xml — SNAPSHOT-equivalent selector

warnings: Probe pivoted from Mode-1 (Ivy resolution) to Mode-3 (binary path scan) because the bolt-4 SCM scanner does not honor ant.ivyResolveDependencies=true from repo-root whitesource.config.

Dynamic revision (catalog #11): rev='3.+' on commons-lang3. Pre-committed JAR is 3.14.0 — the concrete latest-3.x on Maven Central at generation time (2026-05-25). If Mend emits '3.+' instead of '3.14.0', expected_dependency_pairs assertion will fail — this is the intended regression signal.

Changing revision (catalog #23): rev='latest.integration' changing='true' on commons-text. Pre-committed JAR is 1.10.0 — the stable substitute for what SNAPSHOT-equivalent resolution would produce. If Mend emits 'latest.integration' instead of '1.10.0', expected_dependency_pairs assertion will fail.
All four JARs in lib/ are committed; Mode-3 treats all as flat direct deps with no transitive tree structure.
SHA1 values verified against Maven Central published checksums.
